input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 (recommended) or Build Tools

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
```

Then add the required workload components via the VS Installer, **or** do it from the command line:

```powershell
# Modify the existing install to add MAUI + Android
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## 4. Install / Configure Android SDK

VS installs one under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk`. If you need standalone control:

```powershell
# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Discover available platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object | Select-Object -Last 5

& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "build-tools;" | Sort-Object | Select-Object -Last 5

# Install what you need (example — substitute discovered versions)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# Discover available device definitions
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list device |
  Select-String "Name:"

# Create an AVD (substitute the system-image you installed)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK Target

```powershell
# Ensure Developer Mode is on (required for WinUI/MAUI on Windows)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 -t:Run
```

## 8. Discover Versions Dynamically (Cheat Sheet)

```powershell
dotnet --list-sdks                          # Installed .NET SDKs
dotnet workload list                        # Installed workloads
dotnet workload search maui                 # Available MAUI workloads
dotnet new list maui                        # Available templates

# Android SDK versions
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Check the TFMs your project supports
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' *.csproj
```

## Key Notes

- **Hyper-V / WHPX**: Enable for fast Android emulation — `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`.
- **JDK**: The MAUI workload bundles a JDK. If builds complain, set `JAVA_HOME` to the one under `%ProgramFiles%\Microsoft\`.
- Substitute `net9.0` with whatever `dotnet --list-sdks` shows as your major version.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
